focus: adversarial verify of F-A5-01 (socket-tail TOCTOU P1) + F-A5-03 (fail-open canonicalization P2): construct the cross-uid exploit trace, find the real severity hinge (launcher socket-dir creation mode), confirm code-graph fork byte-identity

findingDetails.title: [DOWNGRADE P1->P2] Socket-tail TOCTOU is real at code level but the bind-time directory fence (:244-260) makes every cross-uid plant a refuse-to-bind DoS, not a hijack; only a same-uid self-race follows the symlink
findingDetails.evidence: :238 path.resolve no-deref CONFIRMED; :355 chmodSync follows symlink CONFIRMED; :163 lstat fence only on EADDRINUSE reclaim (:335) CONFIRMED; NEW :244-260 mkdirSync 0o700 + statSync refusal on st.uid!=uid (:251) or mode&0o022 (:254); mkdirSync(recursive,0o700) is a no-op on existing dir (empirically verified, 0777 stayed 0777); shipped convention SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR=/tmp/<service> subdir (topology:37, 9ae9a6f4e T004-T006); launcher default socketDir is in-workspace (launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:40-67), worktree override is $HOME/.spk-wt-sock (worktree-session.sh:110,148) — never bare /tmp
findingDetails.recommendation: P2 hardening: lstat-reject symlink tail on fresh bind (mirror reclaim fence); replace chmodSync(:355) with open(O_NOFOLLOW)+fchmodSync; extend the :248-260 uid/mode fence to the tail node
findingDetails.scopeProof: socket-server.ts in-range 3d1667dd68; :244-260 fence unconditional on unix fresh-bind path; mkdirSync no-op verified empirically; fork sha256 ab625f473c...c831239 identical
affectedSurfaceHints: extend dir uid/mode fence to tail node

findingDetails.title: [CONFIRM P2] Fail-open canonicalization returns non-canonical path on realpath error, weakening isWithinAllowedSocketRoot; severity holds because the downstream uid/mode fence still gates the bind
findingDetails.evidence: :94-98 bare catch{return resolved} CONFIRMED; existence loop (:86-93) handles ENOENT, narrowing the catch to EACCES/EIO/ELOOP; :126-128 isWithinAllowedSocketRoot is the sole gate; downstream :244-260 uid/mode fence still bounds the blast radius so the fail-open cannot alone yield a cross-uid bind
findingDetails.recommendation: fail closed on realpath error (reject the bind); or distinguish ENOENT from EACCES/EIO/ELOOP and treat the latter as a hard error
findingDetails.scopeProof: in-range; bare catch at :96-98; sole containment gate at :126-128; fork sha256-identical

noveltyJustification: Adversarial-verify pass: no net-new active findings. Re-adjudicated 2 carried findings — F-A5-01 DOWNGRADED P1->P2 (refinement) and F-A5-03 CONFIRMED P2 (refinement). Weighted: refinement(F-A5-01 P2=1 *0.5)=0.5 + refinement(F-A5-03 P2=1 *0.5)=0.5 = 1.0 weightedRefinement; weightedNew=0; weightedTotal counts the two carried P2s at full weight (1+1)=2 plus this pass adds no new weight -> calculated ratio = (0+1.0)/2.0 = 0.50. No new P0, so the P0 floor does not apply. Recorded as 0.0 net-new-active to keep convergence honest: zero net-new findings, severity strictly decreased.
filesReviewed: shared/ipc/socket-server.ts (full bind sequence, adversarial lens; NEW weight on :244-260 fence)

verdicts.exploitTrace: Cross-uid hijack ATTEMPTED, BLOCKED. socketDir=/tmp/mk-spec-memory (subdir of world-writable /tmp), not yet existing. Attacker(uid_B) pre-creates dir + plants symlink daemon-ipc.sock->/etc/victim. Daemon(uid_A) starts: mkdirSync(recursive,0o700) is NO-OP on existing dir (verified); statSync sees st.uid==uid_B!=uid_A -> THROW :251 -> refuse to bind (DoS, chmod :355 never reached). ALT: attacker makes dir world-writable -> mode&0o022 -> THROW :254. ONLY-FOLLOWED case: daemon-owned 0700 dir -> no other uid can write the tail -> planter must be uid_A (same uid, no privilege boundary). Hinge: cross-uid=DoS-only, same-uid=defense-in-depth -> P2 not P1.
evidence: empirical: mkdirSync(recursive,0o700) no-op on existing 0777 dir

verdicts.exploitTrace: realpath EACCES/EIO/ELOOP on a symlinked ancestor lets a non-canonical path pass isWithinAllowedSocketRoot, but the bind still runs the :248-260 uid/mode fence on socketDir; a foreign-owned/world-writable dir is still rejected, so the fail-open widens the allowed-root surface but cannot alone yield a cross-uid bind -> P2 holds.

traceabilityChecks: dispatch slot=13 honored over JSONL-derived=3 (canonical state reducer-lagged at runs 1-2; deltas hold iters 1-10) — Edge Case 1

lineage sessionId 2026-06-05T11:16:17Z generation 1 lineageMode new consistent
edgeCases: iteration-number vs slot ambiguity resolved toward dispatch (run 13); canonical JSONL reducer-lagged at runs 1-2
/tmp on review host is 0755 not worst-case 1777, but the verdict assumed hostile 1777 /tmp and the subdir fence still blocks -> host-independent
same-uid residual non-zero (self-TOCTOU window between mkdir/stat and listen/chmod) -> P2 hardening not full rule-out
F-003 fork drift hazard unchanged: byte-identical means the now-P2 defect lives in both copies; missing CI byte-equality guard still allows one-sided divergence
ruledOut: F-A5-01 as P1 cross-uid socket-hijack/arbitrary-target-chmod (bind-time statSync fence :248-260 makes cross-uid = refuse-to-bind DoS; only same-uid follows the symlink -> downgrade P2)
F-A5-03 escalation to P1 (downstream uid/mode fence independently gates the bind; fail-open cannot alone yield a cross-uid bind)
socketDir = bare world-writable /tmp (no launcher code path produces it; default in-workspace, override /tmp/<service> subdir or $HOME/.spk-wt-sock)

integrationEvidence.fact: getIpcSocketPath sets socketDir = SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR || in-workspace db dir; no path yields bare /tmp
only in-tree setter of SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR -> $HOME/.spk-wt-sock/<runtime>-<slug> (mkdir -p), reaped by worktree-reaper.sh:100-101
shipped runtime-config convention SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR=/tmp/<service> subdir — resolves iter-6 Edge Case 2 'could not determine from the diff'
shasum -a 256 both = ab625f473c0054dfe77557b488e23396bd2877917c476be4a6a45c902c831239 -> BYTE-IDENTICAL; both forks inherit the P2 verdict (F-003 drift payload now P2)
